title: Senior Relationship Manager, Trade Finance (Commodities / RCEP)
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking an experienced Relationship Manager to provide comprehensive coverage for a portfolio of commodity clients across the RCEP region. The role focuses on trade finance execution, credit facility management, and cross-selling of corporate banking solutions, while ensuring full compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage and grow a portfolio of commodity and corporate clients across the RCEP region, serving as the primary relationship contact.

  • Execute end-to-end account management processes, including new account onboarding, account maintenance, periodic reviews, and client due diligence (KYC/CDD, AML screening, risk assessment questionnaires).

  • Structure, execute, and monitor trade finance transactions, ensuring compliance with bank policies and regulatory requirements.

  • Oversee timely renewal and maintenance of credit facilities in coordination with credit, risk, and head office teams.

  • Identify and drive cross-selling opportunities across trade finance, corporate lending, cash management solutions, and treasury products.

  • Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders (Operations, Compliance, Credit, Risk, Head Office) to ensure documentation completeness and adherence to internal guidelines.

  • Manage clients’ ongoing enquiries and transactions with prompt, professional responses to maintain high service standards.

  • Maintain daily trackers, monitor transaction statuses, and follow up on outstanding items to ensure smooth execution.

  • Support ad-hoc projects and initiatives as assigned, demonstrating flexibility and problem-solving capabilities.

Job Requirements

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Banking, Finance, Business, or a related discipline.

Experience

  • Minimum 8 years of relevant experience in corporate banking, trade finance, or relationship management.

  • Strong exposure to trade finance products, preferably within commodities or corporate banking segments.

Skills & Knowledge

  • Solid understanding of trade finance, corporate banking products, and credit processes.

  • Strong client management and stakeholder coordination skills.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).

  • Able to work effectively under pressure in a KPI-driven environment.

postedCompany.description:
At GreenLane Talent Acquisition, we redefine the recruitment experience for both clients and job seekers. As a leading talent acquisition partner, we specialize in connecting top-tier professionals with leading companies. Our comprehensive approach covers everything from precise position identification to seamless onboarding, ensuring a perfect fit for both employers and candidates.


For job seekers, GreenLane is more than a recruitment agency; we are your career advocate. Our team is dedicated to understanding your unique skills, aspirations, and preferences, guiding you towards opportunities that align with your career goals. We believe in the transformative power of exceptional talent, and we are here to support you in achieving your professional ambitions.


Whether you are an employer seeking top talent or a job seeker looking for the next career move, GreenLane Talent Acquisition is your trusted partner on the journey to success.
